news.headline: DeFi TVL drops 27% to $156B in Q1, AI, social apps grow. Largest TVL blockchain ETH falls 37%, AI user wallets hit 2.6M. NFT volume down 25% to $1.5B.
news.summary: The total value locked in DeFi protocols dropped to $156 billion in Q1 of 2025 due to economic uncertainty and a major exchange hack. The TVL for Ethereum fell 37% to $96 billion, while Sui saw a 44% drop to $2 billion. Solana, Tron, and Arbitrum also experienced over 30% decreases in TVL. However, Berachain was the only top-10 blockchain to increase, reaching $5.17 billion in TVL. On the other hand, AI and social apps saw growth with a 29% increase in daily unique active wallet interactions. NFT and GameFi protocols regressed in Q1, with NFT trading volume falling by 25% to $1.5 billion. Notably, OKX's NFT marketplace led in sales with $606 million, while Pudgy Penguins NFTs were the top-selling collectibles at $177 million. CryptoPunks remained popular with $63.6 million earned from 477 sales.

news.headline: Consumers may face price hikes on produce, seafood, sugar, and coffee due to tariffs. Importers and retailers are uncertain of the impact.
news.summary: Consumers may soon experience increased prices for seafood, sugar, and coffee as well. However, the full impact of these new tariffs on importers and retailers may not be immediately discernible.

news.headline: Consumers to face tariff impact on groceries; higher prices possible for produce, seafood, sugar, and coffee. Effects still uncertain.
news.summary: In the near future, consumers may experience increased prices for seafood, sugar, and coffee. Importers and retailers are likely to face challenges in determining the impact of these new fees on the market.

Douglas Emmett (NYSE: DEI) to release Q1 2025 earnings on May 6, with a live conference call on May 7 at 11:00 AM PT.
news.summary: In Santa Monica, California, Douglas Emmett, Inc., a real estate investment trust listed on the NYSE under the symbol DEI, disclosed intentions to unveil its first-quarter earnings for 2025 post-market closure on Tuesday, May 6, 2025. A live conference call has been slated for the subsequent day, Wednesday, May 7, 2025, commencing at 11:00 a.m. Pacific Time or 2:00 p.m. Eastern Time. President and Chief Executive Officer Jordan Kaplan, alongside Chief Financial Officer Peter Seymour, will spearhead the call.

news.headline: Charlie Javice, facing a fraud charge, worried about teaching Pilates with an ankle monitor.
news.summary: Charlie Javice, who has been found guilty of fraud, could potentially face several decades in prison. Javice has expressed worries about how an electronic monitoring device would impact her employment situation.

Stay informed about the newest additions on IBD 50, IPO Leaders, and other notable lists showcasing the best stocks in the market. news.headline: Trump's tariff hikes aim to transform US economy, impacting businesses and stocks significantly.
news.summary: President Trump is intensifying tariff increases in an effort to significantly alter the economy. This move carries substantial implications for American businesses and the stock market.

news.headline: 35-year industry-academic partnership renewed between La Jolla Institute for Immunology and Kyowa Kirin, leading to groundbreaking advances in science and patient care, with a focus on drug discovery.
news.summary: The recently agreed-upon partnership extension continues one of the most enduring alliances in drug discovery worldwide. Noteworthy breakthroughs resulting from this collaboration have produced new drug candidates, such as an immunotherapy designed for patients with moderate to severe atopic dermatitis which is now undergoing Phase III clinical trials. The refreshed emphasis is on cell-related projects, highlighting a strategic shift towards innovative research areas. This agreement signifies an ongoing commitment to advancing pharmaceutical research and development, fostering potential for further groundbreaking discoveries in the field.

PointsKash Inc. partners with Discover® Network to offer loyalty rewards debit cards from KashPoint kiosks.
news.summary: In Boca Raton, Florida, on April 3, 2025, PointsKash Inc. revealed their recent deal with Discover® Network, a prominent international payment network, to issue pre-paid loyalty rewards debit cards to PointsKash clients via their KashPoint ATM/BTM kiosks across the country.

news.headline: Study.com is relieving college loan debt for four students as part of its debt payoff program.
news.summary: The recipients of Study.com's College Debt Payoff Program were revealed in an announcement made in Mountain View, California on April 3, 2025. Study.com, known for its acclaimed online educational resources covering K-12 curriculum, college classes, and exam readiness, selected four fortunate individuals to have their student loan debts cleared as part of the program.

news.headline: Mesoblast's allogeneic cell therapy products declared 'U.S. Country of Origin', exempt from tariffs. Products like Ryoncil® and Revascor® not affected.
news.summary: On April 3, 2025, Mesoblast, a leading provider of allogeneic cellular medicines for inflammatory conditions, shared an update regarding its products amid the U.S. Government's recent tariff declaration for imported goods. Mesoblast is confident that its range of allogeneic cellular treatments, which encompass Ryoncil® and Revascor®, will remain unaffected by these tariffs. Mesoblast's standing as a global leader in this sector, listed on Nasdaq under MESO and ASX under MSB, positions it to navigate potential impacts on imported products successfully.

news.headline: Abcourt finalizes first round of private placement, raising $1,668,040.
news.summary: In recent developments, it has been reported that a total of 1,200 individuals were affected by the recent data breach. The incident occurred due to a cyber attack on the company's database, exposing sensitive information such as passwords and personal details. The company has assured all affected customers that they are taking necessary steps to enhance their cybersecurity measures and prevent such breaches in the future. Additionally, the company is offering free credit monitoring services to all individuals whose information was compromised. This breach serves as a reminder of the importance of constantly updating security protocols to safeguard against cyber threats.

The partnership is set to enhance offerings in these key regions and improve the availability of these products and services for businesses in the area. This collaboration signifies a significant step forward for DuraServ in catering to the industrial needs of customers in these important markets.

Helmerich & Payne, Inc. to host fiscal Q2 2025 conference call on May 8th at 11:00 a.m. (ET) with John Lindsay and Kevin Vann. Details on hpinc.com.
news.summary: Helmerich & Payne, Inc. (NYSE: HP) will hold a conference call on May 8, 2025, at 11:00 a.m. (ET) in Tulsa, Oklahoma. The call, in partnership with HP, will feature John Lindsay, President and CEO, Kevin Vann, Senior Vice President and CFO, and Dave Wilson, Vice President of Investor Relations. The fiscal second quarter 2025 earnings release and presentation will be posted on hpinc.com for investors to access.

Barstool Sports founder Dave Portnoy questions why Bitcoin, a supposedly independent asset, mirrors stocks.
news.summary: Dave Portnoy, the founder of Barstool Sports, recently raised concerns about Bitcoin. He questioned why the cryptocurrency, which is known for its independence from traditional financial systems, was mirroring the movements of stocks. Portnoy's remarks have sparked discussion within the cryptocurrency community as Bitcoin's price fluctuates in correlation with stock market trends. The comparison between Bitcoin and stocks has drawn attention to the evolving nature of digital assets and their interconnectedness with the broader financial landscape. This development comes amid growing interest in cryptocurrency, with Bitcoin reaching a market value of over $1 trillion and attracting both retail and institutional investors.

news.headline: Rio Tinto boosts supplier spend in Australia to A$17.7 billion in 2024, up 9.9% y-o-y, benefiting 6,000 suppliers.
news.summary: Rio Tinto has significantly increased its spending with Australian businesses in 2024, surpassing A$17.7 billion. This marks a 9.9% rise from the previous year and involves transactions with over 6,000 suppliers nationwide. The investment has had a positive impact on local, regional, and national economies by creating employment opportunities and supporting Australian-owned companies. Notably, more than A$926 million was included in this spending initiative.

Annexon presents Phase 3 data for ANX005 at AAN 2025 meeting, showing rapid benefit for GBS patients.
news.summary: The upcoming oral plenary presentation on Tuesday, April 8 will showcase Phase 3 data for ANX005, which is noted as the first potential targeted therapy for GBS. The data reveals a swift and long-lasting benefit across various clinical measures and time points. This significant development marks a crucial advancement in combating GBS, offering hope for improved treatment outcomes.
